issues.summary: tagging package imports indexer.LuceneIndexer, reversing the natural pipeline dependency direction
evidence: ConllUProcessor.java line 5: import pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.indexer.LuceneIndexer; line 37: private final LuceneIndexer indexer;
CorpusProcessor.java line 5: import pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.indexer.LuceneIndexer; line 23: private final LuceneIndexer indexer;
The tagging layer produces tokens; the indexer consumes them — the coupling should be mediated by an interface or callback, not a direct import of the concrete indexer
issues.suggestion: Introduce a TokenSink or DocumentConsumer interface in a shared or indexer package that LuceneIndexer implements. Have ConllUProcessor and CorpusProcessor depend on the interface rather than the concrete class. This restores the correct dependency direction and allows tagging to be tested without a live Lucene index.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer dumps 59 System.out.println traces to stdout during every API exploration request
evidence: exploreByPattern() begins with System.out.println("\n=== SEMANTIC FIELD EXPLORATION ===") and then prints each collocate with its logDice score, each intermediate noun count, and full result tables to stdout
explore() duplicates this pattern with another 30+ println calls covering identical step-trace output
SemanticFieldExplorer is called from WordSketchApiServer.handleSemanticFieldExplore() during HTTP request handling — every client API call produces this stdout flood
59 total System.out.println calls in the file; SLF4J logger is declared but unused in this class
issues.suggestion: Replace all System.out.println calls in SemanticFieldExplorer with logger.debug()/logger.info() using SLF4J (the dependency is already on the classpath and logger is used correctly throughout the rest of the codebase). Step-trace output at INFO or DEBUG level will be suppressible in production without code changes.

issues.summary: exploreByPattern() labels two separate code sections 'Step 3', producing confusing doubled output
evidence: Line 179: '// Step 3: Score nouns by shared collocate count' / System.out.println("\nStep 3: Scoring nouns by shared " + relationName + "...") — scores DiscoveredNoun list
Line 206: '// Step 3: Score nouns by shared collocate count' / System.out.println("\nStep 3: Scoring nouns by shared collocates...") — computes CoreAdjective frequency map
The second block is logically Step 4 (it processes the output of the first block); the duplicate label is a copy-paste artifact from an earlier method version
issues.suggestion: Renumber the second block to 'Step 4: Identifying core collocates...' to accurately reflect the algorithm stages. This also removes the duplicate comment on line 206.

issues.summary: Main.showUsage() prints the 'Commands:' help section twice verbatim
evidence: Line 79: System.out.println("Commands:") opens the first Commands block listing blacklab-index, blacklab-query, and server
Line 112: System.out.println("Commands:") opens an identical second block with the same three commands — 34 lines of duplicated println calls
The section from line 112 to ~130 is a verbatim repeat of lines 79-98, inserting a 'Commands:' heading mid-usage-string after the Examples section
issues.suggestion: Remove the duplicated Commands block (lines 112-130). The Examples section at lines 100-111 can close the usage string; a single Commands block at the top is sufficient.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPredicates() is a pure forwarding wrapper with no added logic
evidence: exploreByPredicates(seed, topPredicates, nounsPerPredicate, minShared, minLogDice) immediately calls return explore(seed, topPredicates, nounsPerPredicate, minShared, minLogDice) with identical argument order
No argument validation, no parameter transformation, no default injection — the method simply re-routes the call
The Javadoc says 'Explore using adjectival predicates (best for abstract nouns like theory, model)' but the implementation offers nothing beyond what explore() already does
issues.suggestion: Delete exploreByPredicates() and have callers call explore() directly. If a future overload with different defaults is needed, add it with explicit defaults rather than a pass-through.

issues.summary: CollocationEntry has multi-line Javadoc on trivial 1-2 line delegating methods that purely restate the method name
evidence: '/** Get the number of collocates. */ public int size() { return collocates.size(); }' — the doc adds nothing over the method signature
'/** Check if entry is empty. */ public boolean isEmpty() { return collocates.isEmpty(); }' — identical pattern
'/** Get top N collocates. */ public List<Collocation> topN(int n) { return collocates.stream().limit(n).toList(); }' — same pattern; the parameter name 'n' already communicates this
issues.suggestion: Remove the Javadoc from size(), isEmpty(), and topN(). Reserve Javadoc for methods where the name alone does not communicate the contract (e.g., filterByLogDice where the threshold semantics are worth documenting).

issues.summary: Three generations of indexer coexist (LuceneIndexer, HybridIndexer, BlackLabConllUIndexer) with only the third active from Main.java
evidence: Main.java routes all commands through BlackLabConllUIndexer / BlackLabQueryExecutor exclusively; no command dispatches to LuceneIndexer or HybridIndexer.
CorpusBuilder.java, tagging/ConllUProcessor.java, and tagging/CorpusProcessor.java all instantiate LuceneIndexer and form a complete first-generation pipeline that is unreachable from Main.java.
HybridIndexer.java javadoc explicitly describes itself as 'Unlike the legacy token-per-document indexer' — confirming it was the second generation — but it too is orphaned from the main entry point.
issues.suggestion: Delete the LuceneIndexer-based pipeline (LuceneIndexer.java, CorpusBuilder.java, tagging/ConllUProcessor.java, tagging/CorpusProcessor.java) and, if the hybrid Lucene path is no longer needed, also HybridIndexer.java. Add a brief comment to BlackLabConllUIndexer naming it the canonical indexer. If the Postgres sampler (CorpusBuilder) is still useful, rewrite it to use BlackLabConllUIndexer.

issues.summary: HybridConllUProcessor and SinglePassConlluProcessor both wrap HybridIndexer with overlapping functionality, with no deprecation marker on the older one
evidence: Both classes have identical constructor signatures: HybridConllUProcessor(String indexPath) and SinglePassConlluProcessor(String indexPath), both instantiate HybridIndexer, and both offer processFile() and processDirectory().
SinglePassConlluProcessor javadoc states 'This class no longer produces collocations.bin since the CollocationsBinWriter... have been removed' — explicitly signalling it replaced the older class.
Neither class is marked @Deprecated; a developer cannot tell which to use or extend.
issues.suggestion: Mark HybridConllUProcessor with @Deprecated and add a Javadoc @deprecated tag pointing to SinglePassConlluProcessor. Then, if no external callers remain, delete HybridConllUProcessor in the next cleanup pass.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer.toLegacyRelationType() is defined but never called — a dead migration shim
evidence: toLegacyRelationType() is defined at line 1191 of WordSketchApiServer.java.
Searching the entire src/ tree finds zero call-sites for this method outside its own definition.
The name 'toLegacy' explicitly signals this was a compatibility bridge written during migration, never cleaned up.
issues.suggestion: Delete the toLegacyRelationType() method from WordSketchApiServer.java. If relation-type mapping is ever needed, it belongs on the QueryExecutor.RelationType enum itself (e.g., a static fromString() factory).

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig.isLegacyFormat() and its associated tag= compatibility parsing have zero callers
evidence: isLegacyFormat() is defined at line 559 of GrammarConfigLoader.java with no call-sites found in src/.
The tag= fallback parsing block at lines 607-614 is dead code dependent on the same unreachable branch.
The method's own Javadoc confirms it exists only for backward compatibility with 'legacy: cql_pattern like [tag=NN.*]'.
issues.suggestion: Remove isLegacyFormat() and the tag= fallback branches (lines 607-614) from GrammarConfigLoader.java. If legacy grammar configs using tag= attributes still need to be supported, add a validation step at config load time that rejects them with a clear migration message.

issues.summary: LexiconFromStatsMigrator references CollocationsBuilderV2 which does not exist in the codebase
evidence: Line 17 of LexiconFromStatsMigrator.java: 'After running this, CollocationsBuilderV2 can use the lexicon for frequency lookups' — CollocationsBuilderV2 does not exist in the codebase.
This ghost reference suggests the migration from CollocationsBuilder to a V2 was planned but never completed, and the old migrator was never updated or removed.
issues.suggestion: Either delete LexiconFromStatsMigrator.java entirely if it is no longer needed (the BlackLab pipeline does not use lexicon.bin), or update the Javadoc to remove the CollocationsBuilderV2 reference and document the actual current consumer of the generated lexicon.bin.

issues.summary: indexer/hybrid/ (22 files) mixes 5 distinct concern groups with no sub-package separation
evidence: 22 Java files in a single flat directory, exceeding the >10 mixed-concern threshold.

Five distinct concern groups identified: (1) processing pipeline — HybridIndexer, HybridConllUProcessor, SinglePassConlluProcessor, CollocationsBuilder; (2) domain model records — Collocation, CollocationEntry, TermStatistics, SentenceDocument; (3) codec/binary-IO — LemmaIdsCodec, LemmaLexiconReader, LemmaLexiconWriter, PairRunIO, TokenSequenceCodec, IntArrayBuffer; (4) statistics — StatisticsIndexBuilder, StatisticsReader, StatisticsConverter; (5) utilities/migration — LexiconFromStatsMigrator, LongIntHashMap, LongIntPairSort, LemmaIdAssigner, PositionalTokenStream.
CollocationsBuilder alone is 1139 LOC sitting adjacent to tiny data-carrier records (Collocation at ~40 LOC), making importance and ownership opaque.
issues.suggestion: Stage a reorg in three passes: (1) Create indexer/hybrid/model/ and move Collocation.java, CollocationEntry.java, TermStatistics.java, SentenceDocument.java — update package declarations and all imports (only internal to indexer/hybrid and CollocationsBuilder need updating). Validate: mvn compile. (2) Create indexer/hybrid/codec/ and move LemmaIdsCodec.java, LemmaLexiconReader.java, LemmaLexiconWriter.java, PairRunIO.java, TokenSequenceCodec.java, IntArrayBuffer.java. Validate: mvn compile. (3) Create indexer/hybrid/stats/ and move StatisticsIndexBuilder.java, StatisticsReader.java, StatisticsConverter.java; move LongIntHashMap.java, LongIntPairSort.java, LexiconFromStatsMigrator.java, LemmaIdAssigner.java, PositionalTokenStream.java to indexer/hybrid/util/. Validate: mvn test. The top-level processing classes (HybridIndexer, CollocationsBuilder, HybridConllUProcessor, SinglePassConlluProcessor) remain directly under indexer/hybrid/ as they are the primary entry points.

issues.summary: query/ (15 files) conflates predicate primitives, domain model, executor implementations, and semantic analytics
evidence: Seven predicate combinator classes (AndPredicate, OrPredicate, NotPredicate, LemmaPredicate, TagPredicate, PosGroupPredicate, WordPredicate) plus the TokenPredicate interface are micro-classes (each <30 LOC) that form a self-contained predicate algebra, yet live alongside 887-LOC BlackLabQueryExecutor and 889-LOC SemanticFieldExplorer.
SemanticFieldExplorer implements a bootstrapped semantic-class discovery algorithm — a different concern layer (analytics/exploration) from the raw query execution infrastructure in the same package.
Token and TokenWindow are domain model objects used across predicates and verifiers; they have no ownership signal differentiating them from executor-level utilities.
issues.suggestion: Two-step reorg: (1) Create query/predicate/ and move AndPredicate.java, OrPredicate.java, NotPredicate.java, LemmaPredicate.java, TagPredicate.java, PosGroupPredicate.java, WordPredicate.java, TokenPredicate.java, Token.java, TokenWindow.java — update package declarations and imports in CQLVerifier and any class that constructs predicates. Validate: mvn compile. (2) Move SemanticFieldExplorer.java to a new top-level package query/exploration/ (or promote it to a sibling of query/ as exploration/) since it operates at the application feature level, not the query primitive level. Validate: mvn test.

issues.summary: TestQuery.java at repository root: packageless scratch file with hardcoded Windows path
evidence: TestQuery.java has no package declaration, fan_in=0, fan_out=0 (blind packet root_files entry confirms peripheral role).
Contains hardcoded Windows absolute path: String indexPath = "D:\\corpora_philsci\\bi"; — a development artifact, not a deployable utility.
Imports pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.query.BlackLabQueryExecutor but lives outside the Maven source tree (src/main/java/...), making it uncompilable by standard mvn build.
issues.suggestion: Either delete TestQuery.java (preferred — it is a personal scratch file with no general utility) or migrate it into tools/ as a proper CLI diagnostic: (1) Move to src/main/java/pl/marcinmilkowski/word_sketch/tools/TestQuery.java, (2) add package declaration pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.tools, (3) replace hardcoded path with a CLI argument (args[0]), (4) wire it into Main.java's command dispatch. Validate: mvn compile && git rm TestQuery.java from root if deleted.

issues.summary: corpus/ package (2 files, avg_fan_in=0) is functionally orphaned and misleads navigation
evidence: directory_profiles shows avg_fan_in=0.0 and avg_fan_out=0.0 for corpus/ — nothing in the codebase imports from this package.
CorpusBuilder imports from indexer.LuceneIndexer and tagging.PosTagger, meaning it depends on the main production path but is not itself on any production path (not wired into Main.java).
PostgresCorpusSampler references PostgreSQL JDBC which is not in pom.xml — this is dead experimental code masquerading as a production package.
issues.suggestion: Audit whether corpus/ is intentionally preserved for future use or is dead code. If dead: (1) git rm -r src/main/java/pl/marcinmilkowski/word_sketch/corpus/, (2) verify mvn compile still passes, (3) update README if it references corpus-building from PostgreSQL. If intentionally kept: move to a clearly-named experimental/ or tools/corpus/ sub-package and add a README note marking it as non-production.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer opened per-request in API server, bypassing the shared executor and leaking on exceptions
evidence: WordSketchApiServer.java line ~530: 'SemanticFieldExplorer explorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(indexPath);' — this calls SemanticFieldExplorer(String), which calls 'new BlackLabQueryExecutor(indexPath)' which calls 'BlackLab.open(new File(indexPath))', opening the index from disk on every request.
The same pattern appears at lines ~786 and ~864 for compare and explore-multi paths.
In all three sites close() is called only on the happy path — if 'explorer.exploreByPattern(...)' throws, the index handle is never closed.
WordSketchApiServer already holds 'this.executor' (a shared BlackLabQueryExecutor) which could be passed to the testable 'SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor)' constructor instead.
issues.suggestion: Pass this.executor to SemanticFieldExplorer at all three call sites: 'try (SemanticFieldExplorer explorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(this.executor))'. This reuses the already-open shared index, removes the per-request I/O, and the try-with-resources guarantees close() even on exception paths.

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader.createDefaultEnglish() silently depends on CWD being the project root
evidence: GrammarConfigLoader.java line ~169: 'return new GrammarConfigLoader(Path.of("grammars/relations.json"));' — relative path resolved against process CWD.
Any caller (test runner, tool, future service) that doesn't happen to run from the project root will receive an IllegalStateException with no guidance on what CWD was expected.
The factory is unused in the current codebase, making it a latent trap for the next developer who reaches for a 'convenient default'.
issues.suggestion: Remove createDefaultEnglish() or replace it with a version that resolves the path relative to a caller-supplied base directory or a classpath resource, and document the assumption explicitly: 'public static GrammarConfigLoader createDefault(Path projectRoot)'.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer (1253 lines) mixes HTTP wiring, routing, parameter parsing, JSON serialization, and business logic in one class
evidence: start() registers all routes inline with anonymous lambdas containing non-trivial logic
handleFullSketch, handleRelationQuery, handleFullDependencySketch, handleDependencyRelationQuery are ~50-80 line methods each combining parameter validation, query execution, and JSON assembly
The 6-line WordSketchResult-to-JSONObject serialization block is duplicated verbatim at lines 319-324, 364-369, 418-423, and 469-474
issues.suggestion: Extract a WordSketchResultSerializer helper that converts List<WordSketchResult> to JSONArray. Separate route dispatch (a thin routing class) from handler implementations (one class per handler group, e.g. SketchHandlers, SemanticFieldHandlers). The server class should only wire up the HTTP server and delegate.

issues.summary: QueryExecutor interface is bypassed by 5 explicit BlackLabQueryExecutor downcasts inside the API server
evidence: executor field is declared QueryExecutor but cast to BlackLabQueryExecutor at lines 306, 350, 403, 455, and 694 to call executeSurfacePattern()
executeSurfacePattern() is absent from the QueryExecutor interface, so the abstraction only holds at construction time
SemanticFieldExplorer also contains instanceof BlackLabQueryExecutor guards for the same reason (lines ~120-130)
issues.suggestion: Add executeSurfacePattern() to the QueryExecutor interface (or a richer sub-interface). Alternatively, if only BlackLabQueryExecutor will ever be used, remove the interface indirection and type the field directly; the polymorphism is fictional.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.explore() and exploreByPattern() duplicate the same 3-step semantic field algorithm with ~130 lines of copied logic
evidence: Both methods execute: (1) find collocates for seed, (2) for each collocate find reverse nouns, (3) score nouns by shared-collocate count and logDice, (4) find core collocates
exploreByPattern() prints 'Step 3' twice (lines 179-180 and 206-207), exposing a copy-paste residue
exploreByPredicates() at line 262 is a 2-line delegate that adds no behavior
issues.suggestion: Extract a private exploreFromCollocates(seed, seedCollocScores, ...) that runs steps 2-4 given an already-fetched collocate map. Both explore() and exploreByPattern() differ only in how they fetch step-1 collocates; the rest is identical and should be shared.

issues.summary: CollocationsBuilder.buildRelationCollocations() has ~200 lines of dead code after explicitly disabling itself with Collections.emptyList()
evidence: Line 226: 'simpleRelations = Collections.emptyList()' with log message 'Relation precomputation is DISABLED'
Lines 230-303 iterate over simpleRelations (always empty), execute full parallel ExecutorService/Future infrastructure, and call buildRelationEntriesForLemma—all doing nothing
buildRelationEntriesForLemma() (~60 lines) is only called from this dead path
issues.suggestion: Either delete the method body entirely (keeping only an early-return stub) or, if future re-enabling is intended, remove the live infrastructure and replace with a single TODO comment. Dead code maintained alongside live code signals intent confusion and misleads reviewers.

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader has two near-identical ~40-line methods deriveHeadPositionFromPattern() and deriveCollocatePositionFromPattern() differing only in the label they seek
evidence: deriveHeadPositionFromPattern (line 209) and deriveCollocatePositionFromPattern (line 251) share the same tokenizer loop, skipping logic, and position counter
The only functional difference: head method returns on encountering '1:', collocate method returns on encountering '2:'
Both default to 1 and 2 respectively—same structure, different constant
issues.suggestion: Replace both with a single private static int derivePositionFromPattern(String pattern, int labelNumber) that accepts the label to search for. Call sites become derivePositionFromPattern(pattern, 1) and derivePositionFromPattern(pattern, 2).

issues.summary: The relation-id legacy-name normalization switch is copy-pasted identically into two adjacent handler methods in WordSketchApiServer
evidence: The 4-case switch (adj_modifier->noun_modifiers, adj_predicate->noun_adj_predicates, subject_of->noun_verbs, object_of->verb_nouns) appears at lines 502-507 in handleSemanticFieldExplore and at lines 654-659 in handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti
Any future legacy-name addition or change requires updating both sites
issues.suggestion: Extract a private normalizeRelationId(String id) method and call it from both handlers.

issues.summary: HybridConllUProcessor and tagging/ConllUProcessor share ~50 lines of near-identical CoNLL-U file parsing logic
evidence: Both contain the same UTF-8 lenient decoder setup, blank-line sentence boundary detection, '# text =' comment extraction, and token accumulation loop
Mechanical scan confirmed 51-line boilerplate block shared between the two files
The sentence-building logic (skip '-' tokens, extract word/lemma/upos/xpos/deprel, compute offsets) is similarly replicated
issues.suggestion: Extract a shared CoNLLUReader or CoNLLUIterator utility (e.g., in a parsing subpackage) that yields SentenceTokens objects. Both processors consume the iterator and delegate to their respective indexers. This also makes testing the parser independently straightforward.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer.Builder declares QueryExecutor but four handlers unconditionally cast to BlackLabQueryExecutor, violating the interface contract
evidence: Builder.withExecutor(QueryExecutor executor) at line 63 accepts any QueryExecutor

handleFullSketch (line 306), handleRelationQuery (line 350), handleFullDependencySketch (line 403), handleDependencyRelationQuery (line 455) all cast executor unconditionally via ((BlackLabQueryExecutor) executor).executeSurfacePattern(...)
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti (line 693) correctly guards with 'if (executor instanceof BlackLabQueryExecutor)' before casting, showing the pattern is known but inconsistently applied
executeSurfacePattern and executeDependencyPattern are not declared on the QueryExecutor interface, making them invisible to callers relying on the interface contract
issues.suggestion: Either (a) promote executeSurfacePattern to the QueryExecutor interface (possibly as a default that falls back to findCollocations) so the contract is complete, or (b) change the executor field type to BlackLabQueryExecutor and remove the pretense of interface polymorphism in WordSketchApiServer. The instanceof guard pattern used on line 693 should also be applied uniformly to lines 306, 350, 403, 455 as an immediate safety fix.

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader exposes getRelation() returning Optional<RelationConfig> and getRelationById() returning raw nullable RelationConfig for the same key/map
evidence: GrammarConfigLoader line 313: public Optional<RelationConfig> getRelation(String id) { return Optional.ofNullable(relationsById.get(id)); }
GrammarConfigLoader line 320: public RelationConfig getRelationById(String id) { return relationsById.get(id); } — both look up relationsById with the same key

WordSketchApiServer mixes both: uses getRelationById(relation) at lines 345, 448 (with manual null-check) and getRelation(relationId) at lines 515, 667, 903 (with Optional.isPresent/get) — callers cannot rely on a single nullability contract
issues.suggestion: Remove getRelationById() entirely and standardize callers on getRelation() which returns Optional<RelationConfig>. This eliminates the inconsistent nullability contract and forces callers to handle the absent case explicitly. Update lines 345 and 448 in WordSketchApiServer to use grammarConfig.getRelation(relation).ifPresent(...) or .orElse(null) with explicit intent.

issues.summary: SinglePassConlluProcessor increments sentenceId twice per sentence, producing wrong IDs and double-counted totals
evidence: buildSentence (line 184): 'int id = sentenceId.incrementAndGet();' — increments and uses the value as the document ID.
processFile, inner block (line 101): 'sentenceId.incrementAndGet();' — increments again immediately after buildSentence returns.
processFile, trailing block (line 136): same extra increment for the last sentence in a file.
getSentenceCount() returns sentenceId.get() which is 2×actual, and document IDs will be 1, 3, 5, ... (odd numbers only).
issues.suggestion: Remove the two free-standing 'sentenceId.incrementAndGet()' calls in processFile (lines 101 and 136). The increment inside buildSentence already assigns and records the correct ID. If an external counter for logging is needed, use a separate local variable (e.g., 'fileSentenceCount') which already exists.

issues.summary: extractCollocateFromPattern always returns "unknown" — both branches produce the same value, making the method a no-op
evidence: Lines 482-490: 'int pos2Index = findLabelPosition(bcqlPattern, 2); if (pos2Index <= 0) { return "unknown"; } return "unknown";'
The positive branch computes pos2Index but then returns the same constant regardless of its value.
The comment 'For now, return a placeholder' confirms the body was never completed.
issues.suggestion: Either implement the extraction (iterate over BCQL tokens to the labeled position and return the actual lemma) or, if the method is not yet implemented, make the unimplemented state explicit: throw UnsupportedOperationException, or collapse the method to a single 'return null;' and have callers handle null. As-is, callers receive a silently meaningless value.

issues.summary: exploreByPattern prints 'Step 3' twice for two different logical steps, one of which is actually Step 4
evidence: Line 179-180: '// Step 3: Score nouns by shared collocate count' / 'System.out.println("\nStep 3: Scoring nouns by shared " + relationName + "...");'
Line 206-207: '// Step 3: Score nouns by shared collocate count' / 'System.out.println("\nStep 3: Scoring nouns by shared collocates...");' — a second distinct logical block also labeled Step 3.
The sister method explore() correctly labels the equivalent block as 'Step 4: Identifying core adjectives...' (line 391).
exploreByPattern has no Step 4 announcement despite having four distinct algorithmic phases.
issues.suggestion: Renumber the second block in exploreByPattern from 'Step 3' to 'Step 4' and update its description to 'Identifying core collocates' to match the actual work done and to align with explore()'s step numbering.

issues.summary: Main.showUsage() prints the entire 'Commands:' section twice, sandwiching the Examples section with identical content
evidence: Line 79: 'System.out.println("Commands:");' followed by blacklab-index, blacklab-query, and server descriptions.
Line 112: 'System.out.println("Commands:");' followed by the identical three-command block.
The duplicate block was likely an accidental paste when Examples: was inserted in the middle.
issues.suggestion: Remove the second 'Commands:' block (lines 112 onward through the end of showUsage). The single Commands block printed before the examples is sufficient.

issues.summary: Dead 'return' statement after System.exit(1) in IndexFingerprintTool.main
evidence: Lines 33-35: 'System.exit(1); return;' — return is unreachable because System.exit terminates the JVM.
issues.suggestion: Remove the 'return;' statement. If IDE or linter warnings about unreachable code are a concern, consider throwing an AssertionError instead of System.exit(1) within library-style tools.

issues.summary: RelationConfig.relationType() stores backend discriminator as String instead of an enum
evidence: GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig record: `String relationType` holds exactly two values 'SURFACE' or 'DEP' (line 390).
WordSketchApiServer compares with .equals("SURFACE") at lines 150, 301, 346 and .equals("DEP") at lines 175, 393, 449 — any typo or new value is a silent runtime failure.
GrammarConfigLoader.findRelationIdByType(String relationType) also takes a raw string (line 338) — callers must know the magic strings.
issues.suggestion: Introduce a `RelationBackend` enum with values SURFACE, DEP inside GrammarConfigLoader (or as a top-level type). Change the RelationConfig record field to `RelationBackend relationType`. Parse from JSON once in the loader (RelationBackend.valueOf(str.toUpperCase())) and propagate the enum everywhere — all the .equals() comparisons become switch expressions with exhaustiveness checking.

issues.summary: collocatePosGroup() returns unguarded String POS category instead of an enum
evidence: GrammarConfigLoader.posGroupFromConstraint() returns one of four literal strings: 'adj', 'verb', 'noun', 'adv' or null (lines 598-612).
WordSketchApiServer line 540: `relationConfig.get().collocatePosGroup().equals("adj")` — comparison against magic string; a fifth POS category (e.g., 'pron') added later would silently fall through.
GrammarConfigLoader line 330 filters relations by `headPos.equalsIgnoreCase(r.collocatePosGroup())` with no exhaustiveness guarantee.
issues.suggestion: Define a `PosGroup` enum (ADJ, VERB, NOUN, ADV, OTHER) and change posGroupFromConstraint() to return `PosGroup`. Update all callers to switch on the enum. The null return can become PosGroup.OTHER to eliminate null comparisons.

issues.summary: All API response objects built as Map<String, Object> despite typed domain classes existing
evidence: WordSketchApiServer contains 30+ instances of `Map<String, Object> response = new HashMap<>()` for constructing JSON payloads (lines 545, 718, 790, 868, 928, 1126, etc.).
SemanticFieldExplorer.ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, and CoreAdjective are fully-typed classes yet the API manually copies each field into a Map, e.g. lines 563-614 for the explore response.
There is no compile-time guarantee that a field added to DiscoveredNoun will appear in the API response — the mapping code can silently omit or misspell field names.
issues.suggestion: Annotate the domain result classes (ExplorationResult, DiscoveredNoun, WordSketchResult, etc.) with fastjson2 `@JSONField` or ensure they are serializable by fastjson2's reflection, then pass the typed objects directly to `sendJsonResponse()`. Remove the manual Map-building code. Alternatively, introduce dedicated response record types (e.g., ExploreResponse) that fastjson2 can serialize directly.

issues.summary: @SuppressWarnings("deprecation") in CollocationsBuilder without an explanatory comment
evidence: Line 598: `@SuppressWarnings("deprecation")` on forEachMatchedSentence() with no comment indicating which Lucene API is deprecated, what the migration path is, or when the suppression should be revisited.
issues.suggestion: Add a single-line comment immediately above the annotation: `// Using deprecated BinaryDocValues API; migrate to SortedSetDocValues when upgrading to Lucene 10.x (see LUCENE-XXXXX)`. This makes the intent visible and documents the suppression scope.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern has 10 parameters, well above the API boundary threshold
evidence: exploreByPattern(String seed, int topPredicates, int nounsPerPredicate, int minShared, double minLogDice, String bcqlPattern, String simplePattern, String relationName, int headPos, int collocatePos) — 10 positional parameters at lines 87-97

The last 5 parameters (bcqlPattern, simplePattern, relationName, headPos, collocatePos) are all sourced from GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig at the call site in WordSketchApiServer (lines 534-543), suggesting they should be passed as a single config object
issues.suggestion: Introduce a RelationPattern value object (or reuse the existing GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig) and collapse the last 5 parameters: exploreByPattern(String seed, int topPredicates, int nounsPerPredicate, int minShared, double minLogDice, RelationConfig relation). This also eliminates the parallel string/int reconstruction at every call site.

issues.summary: instanceof BlackLabQueryExecutor downcast used in two files to call executeSurfacePattern, which is absent from the QueryExecutor interface
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer.java line 121: if (executor instanceof BlackLabQueryExecutor) { ((BlackLabQueryExecutor) executor).executeSurfacePattern(...) }
WordSketchApiServer.java line 693: if (executor instanceof BlackLabQueryExecutor) { ((BlackLabQueryExecutor) executor).executeSurfacePattern(...) }

executeSurfacePattern(String lemma, String bcqlPattern, int headPosition, int collocatePosition, double minLogDice, int maxResults) is public on BlackLabQueryExecutor (line 686) but not on QueryExecutor
issues.suggestion: Add executeSurfacePattern (or a more generically named findByPattern) to the QueryExecutor interface with a default implementation that delegates to findCollocations. Remove the instanceof checks from both call sites. This makes the interface honest and allows future executor implementations to override the method properly.

issues.summary: exploreByPredicates is a redundant public alias for explore with identical parameters and no added behaviour
evidence: public ExplorationResult exploreByPredicates(String seed, int topPredicates, int nounsPerPredicate, int minShared, double minLogDice) at line 262 simply calls return explore(seed, topPredicates, nounsPerPredicate, minShared, minLogDice) with no transformation
The method name implies a different mode from explore() but the implementation is identical; parameter names differ only cosmetically (topPredicates vs topAdjectives)
issues.suggestion: Remove exploreByPredicates and let callers call explore() directly. If a semantic distinction is intended (predicates vs adjective-modifier search), implement it as an actual separate code path and document the difference; otherwise the alias adds surface confusion without value.

issues.summary: CorpusProcessor.getTotalTokens() is a public method with an unimplemented body that always returns 0
evidence: public int getTotalTokens() { // This would require tracking, but for now just return 0\n return 0; } — silently returns 0 regardless of tokens indexed
issues.suggestion: Either implement the counter (add a private int totalTokens field, increment in processSentenceBuffer, return it here) or remove the method if it has no callers. A public getter that always returns 0 is a dishonest contract that will mislead any code that tries to use it for progress reporting or validation.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer handler methods create new SemanticFieldExplorer(indexPath) directly rather than using the injected executor, breaking the dependency injection pattern
evidence: Line 530: SemanticFieldExplorer explorer = new SemanticFieldExplorer(indexPath); — creates a new BlackLabQueryExecutor internally, ignoring this.executor
Same pattern at lines 786 and 864 for handleSemanticField and handleSemanticFieldExamples

The Builder explicitly accepts a QueryExecutor via withExecutor(), implying clients can inject test doubles or alternate implementations — but the semantic field handlers silently hardwire to BlackLabQueryExecutor
issues.suggestion: Pass this.executor into the SemanticFieldExplorer constructor: new SemanticFieldExplorer(this.executor). The two-arg constructor already accepts a QueryExecutor. This restores the testability contract implied by the Builder.

issues.summary: sendError() omits Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * header, breaking cross-origin error visibility on every endpoint
evidence: sendJsonResponse() sets exchange.getResponseHeaders().set("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", "*") before writing the body, establishing an open CORS policy for all success responses.
sendError() (lines ~1215-1228) sets Content-Type and the status code but never sets Access-Control-Allow-Origin. All ten registered endpoint contexts call sendError() for 400/405/500 paths.

The result: a browser making a cross-origin request receives a CORS error on any failure path, hiding the actual HTTP error code and JSON error body from the client, while the same request succeeds transparently on the happy path.
issues.suggestion: Add `exchange.getResponseHeaders().set("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", "*");` as the first header line inside sendError(), immediately after the method signature — one-line fix that aligns the error path's trust boundary with the success path's.

issues.summary: CLAUDE.md documents CLI commands (index, conllu, query) that do not exist in Main.java
evidence: CLAUDE.md lines 59-62 document: 'java -jar concept-sketch.jar index --corpus text.txt', 'conllu --input ...', 'query --index ...'
Main.java switch statement handles only: blacklab-index, blacklab-query, server, help — none of the documented commands exist.
issues.suggestion: Update CLAUDE.md 'CLI Commands' section to match Main.java: replace index/conllu/query with blacklab-index/blacklab-query/server, and add the correct option flags shown in Main.showUsage().

issues.summary: indexer/ has three coexisting indexer subsystems with no indication which is active
evidence: Main.java uses only BlackLabConllUIndexer (indexer/blacklab/). indexer/hybrid/ has 20+ classes (HybridIndexer, SinglePassConlluProcessor, CollocationsBuilder, etc.) none of which are reachable from Main or WordSketchApiServer.
tagging/ConllUProcessor and tagging/CorpusProcessor import indexer.LuceneIndexer (the root-level old indexer) and compile as live code.
corpus/CorpusBuilder.java also imports LuceneIndexer. None of these old-path classes are called from any active entry point.
issues.suggestion: Move the dead Lucene/hybrid indexer code to a clearly marked archive (e.g., indexer/legacy/ with a package-info.java stating 'superseded by indexer/blacklab'), or delete it if it is truly unused. Add a package-info.java to indexer/blacklab/ that explicitly states it is the current active backend. This gives a new engineer an immediate signal about what to follow.

issues.summary: 20+ .bak and .tmp.* files are version-controlled inside production source packages, making navigation impossible
evidence: find src/ -name '*.bak' -o -name '*.tmp.*' yields 30+ results across query/, grammar/, indexer/hybrid/, tools/, viz/, and test/ directories.
query/ alone has 10 such files alongside 14 live .java files — a new engineer browsing the package cannot distinguish active from abandoned code without opening each file.
issues.suggestion: Delete all .bak and .tmp.* files from the repository (git rm). Add *.bak and *.tmp.* to .gitignore to prevent future leakage. If any of these files contain useful historical context, the git history preserves them.

issues.summary: TestQuery.java is a debug file tracked at the project root with a hardcoded Windows path
evidence: TestQuery.java line 8: String indexPath = "D:\\corpora_philsci\\bi"; — a Windows-specific absolute path embedded in tracked source.
The file lives at the project root, not in src/test/java, and has no package declaration.
git status shows it as modified (M), meaning it is version-controlled.
issues.suggestion: Either delete TestQuery.java (the use case is covered by integration tests or the CLI) or move it to src/test/java with a proper package and test framework annotations, replacing the hardcoded path with a system property or test-data reference.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer, WordSketchApiServer, and CollocationsBuilder — the three largest critical classes — have zero test coverage
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer is 889 lines and implements the core semantic field exploration feature; no test file references it
WordSketchApiServer is 1253 lines and handles all REST endpoints; no test file references it
CollocationsBuilder is 1139 lines and drives all precomputed collocation ranking; no test file references it
SemanticFieldExplorer already exposes SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor executor) for injection, so testing with a mock is straightforward
issues.suggestion: Add unit tests for SemanticFieldExplorer using a mock QueryExecutor (the constructor already supports it): test exploreByPattern with empty/null seeds, verify ExplorationResult fields, and verify minShared filtering. Add API-level tests for WordSketchApiServer using an embedded HttpServer or a test-only Builder wired to a mock executor. Add at least smoke-level tests for CollocationsBuilder against a tiny in-memory corpus.

issues.summary: DependencySketchTest, BlackLabDummyTest, and BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest silently pass when the required index path is unavailable
evidence: DependencySketchTest line 24: if (!indexDir.exists()) { System.err.println("..."); return; } — six test methods all follow this pattern
BlackLabDummyTest does the same for D:\corpora_philsci\bi
BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest uses @EnabledIf("indexExists") on one method but falls back to the same silent-return pattern on the other
Hardcoded Windows path D:\corpora_philsci\bi will never exist in CI
issues.suggestion: Replace silent return with JUnit 5 @DisabledIf or Assumptions.assumeTrue(indexDir.exists(), "Index unavailable") so the test is reported as skipped rather than passing. Move the index path to an environment variable or system property so CI can report the skip reason. For BlackLabDummyTest, which only prints output with no assertions, either delete it or convert it to a proper integration test with JUnit @Tag("integration") and assertion-based verification.

issues.summary: BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest and DependencySketchTest use raw Java assert statements that are disabled unless JVM runs with -ea
evidence: BlackLabBcqlConcordanceTest lines 49, 56, 60, 64, 89, 97, 101: 'assert r.getSentence() != null ...', 'assert results.size() > 0 ...' — all raw Java asserts
DependencySketchTest lines 50, 166, 192: 'assert results != null', 'assert results.isEmpty()'
Maven Surefire does not enable assertions by default; these checks are silent no-ops in standard mvn test runs
issues.suggestion: Replace all raw 'assert expr : message' with JUnit 5 assertions (assertNotNull, assertTrue, assertEquals). If assertions are intentionally run with -ea, add <argLine>-ea</argLine> to the maven-surefire-plugin configuration in pom.xml and document it.

issues.summary: TestBCQLParser contains only a main() method and is never executed by JUnit
evidence: TestBCQLParser.java has no @Test annotations and no extends/implements of any JUnit base; it is a plain Java program under src/test
Running mvn test will compile it but execute zero tests from this file
The file tests BCQL parsing — a non-trivial code path — but offers no automated verification
issues.suggestion: Convert TestBCQLParser to a proper JUnit 5 test class: rename to BCQLParserTest, annotate each test case with @Test, replace System.out.println with assertions (e.g., assertNotNull(pattern), assertDoesNotThrow(...)), and remove the main() method.

issues.summary: LongIntHashMap, a custom open-addressing hash map used in core indexing, has no tests
evidence: LongIntHashMap.java contains non-trivial rehash logic (lines 100-120), mix64 hash function, and addTo increment semantics
No test file in src/test references LongIntHashMap
The class is used in high-frequency collocation counting loops, making correctness critical
issues.suggestion: Add LongIntHashMapTest covering: addTo with new keys, addTo increment on existing keys, rehash triggered by load threshold (fill past resizeAt), forEach iteration after rehash, and clear() reuse. A parameterized test with a range of initial capacities would also cover the init() rounding logic.

issues.summary: DiagnosticsIntegrationTest.skipDiagnostic() is a placeholder that always passes, providing no coverage
evidence: DiagnosticsIntegrationTest line 28: if (!Files.exists(idx)) { ...; return; } — exits before any assertion when index/ is absent
When index/ is present, the method prints a message and calls assertTrue(true) — no real assertion
Comment reads 'This test is now a placeholder'
issues.suggestion: Either delete this test class entirely, or replace it with a genuine integration test that builds a small in-memory index (using @TempDir) and verifies an observable diagnostic property. If the feature tested was removed, remove the test file to avoid dead test noise in the suite.

issues.summary: SinglePassConlluProcessor uses 'Conllu' while all other CoNLL-U classes use 'ConllU'
evidence: SinglePassConlluProcessor.java line 33: 'public class SinglePassConlluProcessor implements Closeable' — 'Conllu' with lowercase 'u'.
HybridConllUProcessor.java line 29: 'public class HybridConllUProcessor implements Closeable' — 'ConllU' with uppercase 'U'.
ConllUProcessor.java (tagging) and BlackLabConllUIndexer.java also use 'ConllU' with uppercase 'U', making SinglePassConlluProcessor the sole outlier across all four CoNLL-U processing classes.
issues.suggestion: Rename SinglePassConlluProcessor to SinglePassConllUProcessor (capital 'U') to align with the codebase-wide 'ConllU' convention. Update the file name, class declaration, all internal references, and any callers.

issues.summary: tagging package uses SLF4J string concatenation while sibling CoNLL-U processors use placeholder syntax
evidence: tagging/ConllUProcessor.java line 59: logger.info("Processing CoNLL-U file: " + inputFile) — string concatenation.
tagging/CorpusProcessor.java line 40: logger.info("Starting corpus processing: " + inputFile) — string concatenation.
indexer/hybrid/HybridConllUProcessor.java line 60: logger.info("Processing CoNLL-U file: {}", inputFile) — SLF4J placeholder style.
indexer/LuceneIndexer.java uses SLF4J placeholder style throughout, making the tagging package a style island.
issues.suggestion: Convert all string-concatenation log calls in tagging/ConllUProcessor.java and tagging/CorpusProcessor.java to SLF4J placeholder syntax (e.g., logger.info("Processing CoNLL-U file: {}", inputFile)). This eliminates eager string construction on disabled log levels and aligns with the rest of the codebase.

issues.summary: CorpusBuilder.buildIndex() silently swallows all exceptions per sentence with no logging
evidence: Lines 117-119: catch (Exception e) { // Skip problematic sentences } — no logger call, no counter increment, no rethrow; any tagger NPE, IndexOutOfBoundsException, or Lucene failure is invisible to the caller
The caller receives a BuildStats with silently lower sentence/token counts than expected, with no way to distinguish a successful run from a run that skipped 90% of input
issues.suggestion: Add a skippedSentences counter and log a warning per skipped sentence (at minimum log the exception class and message). After the loop, if skippedSentences > 0, log a summary warning: 'Skipped %d sentences due to errors'. This matches the pattern already used in HybridConllUProcessor which logs progress and errors.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor.getTotalFrequency() returns 0 silently on any exception, corrupting logDice scores
evidence: Lines 529-531: catch (Exception e) { return 0L; } — a frequency of 0 makes logDice undefined/negative and causes the collocate to be dropped or ranked incorrectly

All other public methods in the same class (findCollocations, executeQuery, findCollocationsForDeprel) declare 'throws IOException'; getTotalFrequency alone uses a silent fallback, making the contract inconsistent
issues.suggestion: Change to 'catch (Exception e) { throw new IOException("Failed to get frequency for lemma: " + lemma, e); }' to match the IOException contract of sibling methods. Callers that truly need a safe fallback can catch IOException themselves and decide what default to use.

issues.summary: HybridConllUProcessor.buildSentence() silently discards NumberFormatException with no log; ConllUProcessor logs at debug for the same case
evidence: HybridConllUProcessor.java lines 210-212: catch (NumberFormatException e) { // Skip invalid token IDs } — no logger call
ConllUProcessor.java lines 196-199: catch (NumberFormatException e) { logger.debug("Skipping non-numeric token ID: " + fields[0]); } — same pattern with debug log
These two classes perform the same CoNLL-U processing task but handle the same exception differently, making diagnostic behavior unpredictable
issues.suggestion: Add 'logger.debug("Skipping non-numeric token ID '{}' in sentence {}", fields[0], id);' inside the catch block to match ConllUProcessor's approach. Optionally extract the shared token-parsing logic into a shared utility.

issues.summary: HybridIndexer.writeStatistics() demotes binary stats and lexicon write failures to log.warn() with no propagation
evidence: Lines 239-244: catch (Exception e) { log.warn("Failed to write binary statistics: {}", e.getMessage()); } — binary stats file required for query correctness
Lines 250-259: catch (Exception e) { log.warn("Failed to write lexicon: {}", e.getMessage()); } — lexicon.bin required for single-pass collocations
Both files are described in CLAUDE.md as required index components; silently degrading to warn means an indexing run that produces a broken index will appear successful
issues.suggestion: Rethrow as IOException (or log + rethrow) so the caller (HybridConllUProcessor.writeStatistics or Main) can fail fast. At minimum, escalate to log.error() and document explicitly that the index will be incomplete. A broken but apparently successful index run is harder to debug than a clear failure.

issues.summary: CoNLL-U is spelled two different ways across class names in the same package
evidence: HybridConllUProcessor (indexer.hybrid) uses 'ConllU'
SinglePassConlluProcessor (indexer.hybrid, same package) uses 'Conllu' — lowercase 'u'
ConllUProcessor (tagging) uses 'ConllU' matching the first convention
issues.suggestion: Standardise on 'ConlluProcessor' (all-lowercase after 'Conll') or 'ConllUProcessor' (capital U) codebase-wide. The Lucene/Java convention for acronym suffixes favours a single capital letter boundary, so 'ConllUProcessor' is the better choice; rename SinglePassConlluProcessor to SinglePassConllUProcessor.

issues.summary: HybridConllUProcessor and SinglePassConlluProcessor are named along different conceptual axes despite doing the same work in the same package
evidence: 'Hybrid' in HybridConllUProcessor refers to the output index type, not the processing strategy
'SinglePass' in SinglePassConlluProcessor refers to the algorithmic strategy, not the output type
Both classes have identical public APIs (processFile, processDirectory, setCommitInterval, close) and both delegate to HybridIndexer
The Javadoc for SinglePassConlluProcessor notes 'This class no longer produces collocations.bin' suggesting it supersedes HybridConllUProcessor, but a developer cannot infer this from the names
issues.suggestion: If one class supersedes the other, delete the obsolete one and give the survivor a name that expresses the output type consistently, e.g., HybridIndexConlluProcessor or simply HybridConllUIndexer. If both are intentionally kept for different call sites, add a deprecation annotation to the obsolete class and document in its Javadoc which should be used.

issues.summary: ConllUProcessor.processCoNLLUSentence() uses the generic 'process' verb for a method that exclusively indexes tokens into Lucene
evidence: processCoNLLUSentence (line 145) calls indexer.addWord() for every token in the sentence — it indexes, it does not 'process' in any general sense
The dimension rubric explicitly flags 'process' as a generic verb that reveals nothing
issues.suggestion: Rename to indexCoNLLUSentence to match the actual behaviour (indexing tokens into Lucene). This makes the read path obvious: processFile → indexCoNLLUSentence → indexer.addWord.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor has six private methods all named extractCollocateFrom*, leaving callers unable to distinguish their semantics from the name alone
evidence: extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition(xmlSnippet, position) — parses XML attribute at ordinal position
extractCollocateFromMatchText(matchText, position) — splits plain-text tokens by whitespace
extractCollocateFromPattern(bcqlPattern, hitIndex) — returns 'unknown'; placeholder
extractCollocateFromSnippet(snippet) — takes last lemma attribute from XML
extractCollocateFromMatch(matchOnly, labelPos) — (line 762) parses match string by label position
extractCollocateFromPlainText(text, position) — (line 787) splits plain text
Six methods share the same prefix yet differ in input format, return strategy, and completeness
issues.suggestion: Rename to encode the input contract in the suffix: extractLemmaAtPositionFromXml, extractLemmaAtPositionFromTokens, extractLastLemmaFromXmlSnippet, etc. This eliminates the need for callers to read the body to understand which overload applies.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer casts QueryExecutor to BlackLabQueryExecutor 5 times to call executeSurfacePattern(), bypassing the interface abstraction
evidence: Lines 306, 350, 403, 455, 694 of WordSketchApiServer.java all contain ((BlackLabQueryExecutor) executor).executeSurfacePattern(...) — five forced downcasts to reach functionality not declared in the interface.
executeSurfacePattern(String lemma, String bcqlPattern, int headPosition, int collocatePosition, double minLogDice, int maxResults) is defined only on BlackLabQueryExecutor, not on QueryExecutor.

The five handler methods (handleFullSketch, handleRelationQuery, handleFullDependencySketch, handleDependencyRelationQuery, handleSemanticField) all depend on the cast, meaning any alternative QueryExecutor implementation would break these handlers at runtime.
issues.suggestion: Promote executeSurfacePattern() into the QueryExecutor interface (possibly with a default implementation that throws UnsupportedOperationException or delegates to findCollocations). This removes the need for all 5 casts and restores honest interface coverage. Alternatively, introduce a SurfacePatternQueryExecutor sub-interface that extends QueryExecutor and add an instanceof guard, but the cleaner solution is interface promotion.

issues.summary: CollocationsBuilder.buildRelationCollocations() is publicly callable but always produces empty results because the feature was disabled inline
evidence: Line 226: List<GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig> simpleRelations = Collections.emptyList(); — immediately discards the grammarConfig argument.
Comment at lines 223-225: 'Relation precomputation is DISABLED. Word sketches require proper CQL pattern matching (grammatical relations), not window-based co-occurrence.'
The method still iterates simpleRelations (lines 230-242), writes an empty file (line 303), and logs '0 relations' — all work for zero output. It is called from line 1070.
issues.suggestion: Remove buildRelationCollocations() entirely or replace its body with a single documented throw (throw new UnsupportedOperationException("Relation precomputation is disabled — use CQL scan at query time")) and update the call site at line 1070 accordingly. A public method with a silently no-op body misleads callers and wastes build time.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPredicates() is a pure forwarding wrapper over explore() with no added behavior
evidence: Lines 262-270: exploreByPredicates(String seed, int topPredicates, int nounsPerPredicate, int minShared, double minLogDice) contains a single statement: return explore(seed, topPredicates, nounsPerPredicate, minShared, minLogDice) — all 5 parameters forwarded unchanged.
No preconditions, no logging, no parameter adaptation. The method name 'exploreByPredicates' implies distinct semantics from 'explore' but the implementation is identical.
issues.suggestion: Remove exploreByPredicates() and update all callers to call explore() directly. If the name distinction was intended to communicate usage intent, capture that via a Javadoc alias on explore() rather than a forwarding method.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByPattern() takes 10 parameters, mixing domain config with BCQL-specific positional details
evidence: Lines 87-97: exploreByPattern(String seed, int topPredicates, int nounsPerPredicate, int minShared, double minLogDice, String bcqlPattern, String simplePattern, String relationName, int headPos, int collocatePos) — 10 parameters.
headPos and collocatePos are BCQL-specific positional indices that leak the BlackLab pattern format into the semantic exploration API, coupling the caller to executor internals.
WordSketchApiServer (lines 534-540) assembles all 10 arguments individually each time it calls this method, with no grouping.
issues.suggestion: Introduce a PatternRelationConfig value object (or use the existing GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig directly since it already holds bcqlPattern, simplePattern, headPosition, collocatePosition, and relationName) and reduce the signature to exploreByPattern(String seed, ExplorationConfig config, PatternRelationConfig relation). This reduces the signature to 3 parameters and removes the positional index leakage.

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader.RelationConfig has two one-line wrapper methods (extractXposPattern, extractPosPattern) that add no behavior over the method they delegate to
evidence: Line 528-530: extractXposPattern(String constraint) { return extractConstraintAttribute(constraint, "xpos"); } — single-line delegation.
Line 536-538: extractPosPattern(String constraint) { return extractConstraintAttribute(constraint, "tag"); } — single-line delegation.
extractConstraintAttribute(constraint, attrName) is already clear and concise; the wrapper methods add an extra indirection layer with no naming, policy, or transformation value.
issues.suggestion: Inline the two wrapper methods by replacing their call sites with direct calls to extractConstraintAttribute(constraint, "xpos") and extractConstraintAttribute(constraint, "tag") respectively, then delete the wrappers.

issues.summary: fastjson2 explicitly declared while Jackson (jackson-databind + jackson-core) is already on classpath via BlackLab transitive deps
evidence: pom.xml line 41-45: <artifactId>fastjson</artifactId><version>2.0.25</version>
WordSketchApiServer.java imports com.alibaba.fastjson2.JSON, JSONArray, JSONObject
GrammarConfigLoader.java imports com.alibaba.fastjson2.JSON, JSONObject, JSONArray
blacklab-core-pom.xml lists jackson-core:2.13.4, jackson-databind:2.13.4 as BlackLab dependencies — both arrive transitively
issues.suggestion: Replace fastjson2 usages in WordSketchApiServer, GrammarConfigLoader, and IndexFingerprintTool with Jackson ObjectMapper / JsonNode (already on the classpath via BlackLab). Remove the com.alibaba:fastjson dependency from pom.xml. Jackson is battle-hardened and already present — fastjson adds audit surface without adding capability.

issues.summary: Full PostgreSQL JDBC driver (~1.5MB) shaded into fat jar for a single optional utility class
evidence: pom.xml: postgresql:42.7.1 declared with <scope>runtime</scope>; maven-shade-plugin bundles all runtime deps
PostgresCorpusSampler.java: the only class using JDBC; described in its own Javadoc as 'Used for building test corpora from production data' — a one-off utility, not a core feature
CorpusBuilder.java is the only caller of PostgresCorpusSampler
issues.suggestion: Move the PostgresCorpusSampler and CorpusBuilder to a separate Maven module (e.g., corpus-tools) with the postgresql dependency, or make it an optional dependency with provided scope and document that it requires the driver at runtime only when the corpus import feature is needed. This keeps the main shaded jar free of the JDBC driver for the common deployment path.

issues.summary: Core BlackLab dependency resolved from a raw GitHub file URL rather than a stable artifact repository
evidence: pom.xml lines 103-107: repository URL is https://raw.githubusercontent.com/instituutnederlandsetaal/BlackLab/mvn-repo/ — a raw GitHub file path, not a proper Maven repository with redirect or proxy support
BlackLab 4.0.0 is the primary dependency underpinning all corpus query functionality; if this URL becomes unavailable or the branch is moved, the build fails
issues.suggestion: Install blacklab-engine and blacklab-query-parser into a local Maven repository (mvn install:install-file) or a team Nexus/Artifactory proxy, and document this in BLACKLAB_SETUP.md. Alternatively, use the install-blacklab.sh script that already exists and pin that workflow in CI. Remove the raw GitHub repository entry from pom.xml once the artifacts are available through a stable channel.

issues.summary: 30+ System.out.println('DEBUG ...') calls left in production request handlers bypass the established SLF4J logger
evidence: substituteCollocate() contains ~10 System.out.println('DEBUG: ...') statements (lines 982-1006) that are invoked on every concordance-examples request.
handleConcordanceExamples() has 4 debug printlns (lines 907, 911, 913, 916, 923).
handleBcqlQueryPost() has 'System.out.println("DEBUG BCQL: " + bcqlQuery)' on line 1122.
handleVisualRadial() has 2 debug printlns (lines 1061, 1065).
The class already has 'private static final Logger logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(...)' on line 39.
issues.suggestion: Replace all System.out.println('DEBUG ...') calls with logger.debug() calls using the existing SLF4J logger. For substituteCollocate specifically, collapse the 10 println statements to a single logger.debug('substituteCollocate: tokens={}, collocatePos={}', tokens, collocatePosition) call.

issues.summary: Identical 4-field JSONObject collocation builder (lemma, frequency, logDice, pos) copy-pasted four times in WordSketchApiServer
evidence: handleFullSketch() lines 318-325: JSONObject word = new JSONObject(); word.put('lemma', result.getLemma()); word.put('frequency', result.getFrequency()); word.put('logDice', result.getLogDice()); word.put('pos', result.getPos());
Identical block in handleRelationQuery() lines 362-370.
Identical block in handleFullDependencySketch() lines 417-424.
Identical block in handleDependencyRelationQuery() lines 467-474.
issues.suggestion: Extract a private helper: 'private JSONObject toCollocateJson(QueryResults.WordSketchResult r) { JSONObject o = new JSONObject(); o.put("lemma", r.getLemma()); o.put("frequency", r.getFrequency()); o.put("logDice", r.getLogDice()); o.put("pos", r.getPos()); return o; }' and call it in all four sites.

issues.summary: 9-line legacy-relation-ID switch block is copy-pasted verbatim into both handleSemanticFieldExplore and handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti
evidence: handleSemanticFieldExplore lines 502-508: 'relationId = switch (relationId) { case "adj_modifier", "modifier" -> "noun_modifiers"; ...'
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti lines 654-660: identical switch expression with the same four cases.
issues.suggestion: Extract a private helper 'private String resolveRelationId(String rawId) { return switch (rawId.toLowerCase()) { ... }; }' and call it in both handlers.

issues.summary: extractCollocateFromPattern() in BlackLabQueryExecutor always returns 'unknown' regardless of input — dead placeholder shipped in production
evidence: Lines 482-490: 'private String extractCollocateFromPattern(String bcqlPattern, int hitIndex) { int pos2Index = findLabelPosition(bcqlPattern, 2); if (pos2Index <= 0) { return "unknown"; } // For now, return a placeholder - actual extraction would need hit-specific data return "unknown"; }'
The method is defined but its body is entirely a stub — the comment admits it was never implemented.
issues.suggestion: Either implement the method properly using the existing extractCollocateFromXmlByPosition / extractCollocateFromMatch helpers, or delete it and any callers. As-is it misleads readers about capability.

issues.summary: Main.showUsage() prints the 'Commands:' listing twice in the same method body
evidence: Lines 83-92 print 'Commands:' with blacklab-index, blacklab-query, server entries.
Lines 127-136 repeat the same 'Commands:' heading and all three command descriptions verbatim — a copy-paste left over from an earlier edit.
issues.suggestion: Delete the second duplicate block (lines 127-136) from showUsage().

issues.summary: RelationDef inner class and toLegacyRelationType() in WordSketchApiServer are never called — dead remnants
evidence: RelationDef (lines 1238-1252) is declared but no code in the file instantiates it; its constructor sets description = name indicating it was copied from a real use that was later replaced.
toLegacyRelationType() (lines 1191-1202) converts a String to a legacy QueryExecutor.RelationType enum but grep shows no call site within WordSketchApiServer.java.
issues.suggestion: Delete the RelationDef inner class (lines 1238-1252) and the toLegacyRelationType() method (lines 1191-1202).

issues.summary: exploreByPattern() in SemanticFieldExplorer labels two different algorithm steps as 'Step 3', breaking the narrative for readers
evidence: Line 180: 'System.out.println("\nStep 3: Scoring nouns by shared " + relationName + "...");'
Line 206: 'System.out.println("\nStep 3: Scoring nouns by shared collocates...");' — a second Step 3 in the same method, introduced by copy-paste from explore() where it was Step 4.
issues.suggestion: Renumber the second block to 'Step 4' (or merge the duplicate scoring loops). The two Step 3 blocks compute overlapping things (scoring nouns, then scoring core collocates) and the mislabeling hides that the method effectively runs Step 3 twice.

issues.summary: explore() and exploreByPattern() in SemanticFieldExplorer duplicate the same 3-step algorithm skeleton (~200 lines each) instead of factoring the shared structure
evidence: explore() (lines 291-443) and exploreByPattern() (lines 87-257) share identical structure: collect seed collocates into seedCollocScores map, build nounProfiles via reverse lookup, score nouns by shared-collocate count and avgLogDice, identify coreCollocates, print results, return ExplorationResult. The scoring and ranking blocks (discoveredNouns sort, coreCollocates accumulation and sort) are copied verbatim.
The only material difference is how seedCollocScores is obtained (executeSurfacePattern with BCQL pattern vs. findCollocations with ADJECTIVE_PATTERN).
issues.suggestion: Extract the shared skeleton into a private 'ExplorationResult runExploration(String seed, Map<String,Double> seedCollocScores, ...)' method. Both explore() and exploreByPattern() would obtain seedCollocScores via their respective strategies, then delegate to the common method for steps 2-4.

issues.summary: ConcordanceResult exposes 5 constructors with shifting nullable fields, making the query→API boundary unpredictable
evidence: ConcordanceResult has 5 constructors; lemma, tag, word, and rawXml are null in 3 of them, but callers receive the same type regardless of which path created the object
A consumer calling getSentence(), getLemma(), getTag() cannot know which fields are populated without knowing the construction context
The 'full constructor with raw XML' and the 'BlackLab-style results' constructor both populate sentence but leave lemma/tag/word null for different reasons
issues.suggestion: Split ConcordanceResult into two or three purpose-specific result types (e.g., TokenResult for lemma/tag/word-populated results and SnippetResult for concordance-style results), each with only the fields it guarantees to populate. Alternatively, use a sealed interface with distinct subtypes and eliminate the null-field ambiguity entirely.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor has five overlapping regex-based XML extraction helpers at the BlackLab→domain boundary
evidence: extractLemmaFromMatch, extractCollocateFromMatch, extractDependentLemmaFromGroup, extractTokenFromSnippet, and extractPosFromGroup all parse the same BlackLab XML match string using the same LEMMA_ATTR/XPOS_ATTR/UPOS_ATTR patterns
extractCollocateFromMatch and extractTokenFromSnippet are functionally identical (nth regex match from XML) but are separate methods with different parameter names
The plain-text fallback in findCollocations (lastIndexOf(' ')) and extractCollocateFromPlainText in executeSurfacePattern implement the same fallback logic independently
issues.suggestion: Extract a single BlackLabMatchParser class that owns all match-text-to-domain translation. Expose methods like extractLemmaAtPosition(text, n), extractPos(text), extractLastLemma(text). Call this from all three findCollocations / executeSurfacePattern / findByDependencyRelation paths. This consolidates translation at one obvious place and eliminates the parallel partial implementations.

issues.summary: POS-to-group mapping is implemented three times across three files with no shared root
evidence: PosTagger.TaggedToken.getPosGroup() maps Penn Treebank first char (N→noun, V→verb, J→adj, R→adv, D→det, P→pron, I→prep, C→conj, U→punct)
ConllUProcessor.mapUPOStoGroup() maps Universal POS strings (NOUN/PROPN/PRON→noun, VERB→verb, ADJ→adj, AUX→verb, CCONJ/SCONJ→conj) with different groupings
ConllUProcessor.guessUPOS() converts Penn Treebank tags back to Universal POS using yet another first-char switch, creating a round-trip path that is inconsistent with TaggedToken.getPosGroup()
issues.suggestion: Introduce a single PosGroupMapper utility class (or static methods on a shared PosUtils class) with two clearly named factory methods: fromPennTreebank(String xpos) and fromUniversalPos(String upos). Both ConllUProcessor and TaggedToken should delegate to this shared class. This puts tag translation at one obvious place and eliminates the three diverging implementations.

issues.summary: CQLVerifier.verify() and verifyForCollocate() are nearly identical, creating a misleading dual seam
evidence: verify() and verifyForCollocate() have the same method body: build steps, call matchSequence, return VerificationResult
The only documented difference is the comment 'looking for collocates' and 'The headword is at position 0 (conceptually)' — there is no structural difference in the implementation
A caller invoking CQLVerifier cannot determine which method to call; the seam provides no meaningful distinction
issues.suggestion: Delete verifyForCollocate() and route all callers through verify(). If a headword-at-position-zero semantic is needed, make it explicit via a parameter (e.g., verify(pattern, window, headwordPosition)) which already exists, making the method signature self-explanatory.

issues.summary: CorpusProcessor.getTotalTokens() is a public API method that always returns 0
evidence: getTotalTokens() has the comment 'This would require tracking, but for now just return 0' and returns 0 unconditionally
processCorpus() does track totalTokens locally and logs it, but does not persist it to any field
Any caller relying on this method after processCorpus() receives a broken answer from an apparently complete module boundary
issues.suggestion: Add a private int totalTokensProcessed field to CorpusProcessor, increment it inside processSentenceBuffer(), and return it from getTotalTokens(). This is a single-edit fix that makes the API honest.
